title: The Good The Sad And The Drugly

jokes.text: Did you unscrew all the Phillips heads? Check. Did you unscrew all the flat-heads? Yep. And now we wait.
jokes.setup: Two kids having completed some task with screws
jokes.punchline: The casual 'and now we wait' reveals they've sabotaged something for destruction
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart/Milhouse prank setup with perfect deadpan delivery. The methodical checklist followed by casual waiting builds tension nicely.

jokes.text: Now go hide the evidence while I walk around saying, (GASPING) 'What happened? Who would do such a thing?'
jokes.setup: Bart instructing Milhouse after their prank
jokes.punchline: His theatrical fake innocence performance preview
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's calculating nature and love of performance. The gasping preview is funny but predictable for his character.

jokes.text: I'd recognize those discolored Hush Puppies anywhere. Hear that? He thinks my Try 'N Save brand Shush Mutts are genuine Hush Puppies.
jokes.setup: Skinner identifying someone by their shoes
jokes.punchline: Willie's pride in his knockoff 'Shush Mutts' being mistaken for the real brand
jokes.explanation: 'Shush Mutts' is brilliant wordplay - sounds like a real knockoff brand. Willie's misplaced pride in his cheap shoes is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Hard to believe one lad can cause all this mayhem. Particularly a lad who failed Introduction to Unscrewing.
jokes.setup: Skinner puzzling over extensive damage
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Bart failed the most basic skill needed for the prank
jokes.explanation: Nice irony that someone who failed unscrewing class pulled off an elaborate unscrewing prank. Shows the absurdity of school bureaucracy.

jokes.text: And I don't buy this lone prankster theory. I think there was a second fun man.
jokes.setup: Skinner investigating the prank conspiracy
jokes.punchline: JFK assassination parody with 'second fun man' instead of 'gunman'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody turning conspiracy theory language into school prank investigation. 'Second fun man' is perfect - maintains the cadence while being completely absurd.

jokes.text: Milhouse, you realize if you're suspended, you'll be missing Taco Wednesday? Crunchy shell, ground beef, lettuce, mild sauce and, of course, shredded Cheddar cheese. Mmm-mmm.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to get Milhouse to confess
jokes.punchline: His elaborate, sensual description of basic school tacos as torture
jokes.explanation: Skinner's earnest belief that cafeteria tacos are motivating is funny but runs a bit long. The detailed description is both the joke and what makes it drag.

jokes.text: Willie, take him to the big house where he lives.
jokes.setup: Skinner dramatically ordering Milhouse's removal
jokes.punchline: Prison terminology deflated to mean just going home
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic prison language. Economical and well-structured - builds up the drama then punctures it instantly.

jokes.text: Suspended boy walking!
jokes.setup: Willie escorting suspended Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'dead man walking' execution announcement
jokes.explanation: Another prison/execution parody applied to minor school discipline. Willie's dramatic delivery makes it work. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: I'm grounded and spend all day listening to my dad yell at Mad Money w/ Jim Cramer. You said tech stocks were bulletproof!
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining his punishment
jokes.punchline: His dad's financial TV rage as torture
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to Jim Cramer and 2008 financial crisis. Feels a bit forced - the pop culture reference doesn't add much beyond recognition.

jokes.text: You know, I think Milhouse is El Barto.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Milhouse being suspended
jokes.punchline: Her completely wrong guess about Bart's secret identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge obliviousness. She's literally talking to El Barto about who El Barto might be. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: So, Grampa, not like I'm anxious to go, but I've been here 10 minutes, which is like seven hours in kid years.
jokes.setup: Bart visiting the retirement home
jokes.punchline: His calculation of relative time perception
jokes.explanation: Relatable kid logic about time dragging. The 'kid years' concept is clever but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: It's her! The little girl who doesn't recoil at the sight of us!
jokes.setup: Old folks excited about a visitor
jokes.punchline: Their low bar for appreciation - just not being disgusted
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny take on elderly invisibility. The specificity of 'doesn't recoil' is both sad and humorous.

jokes.text: Chocolate chip for Donald. Low sodium for Abe. Not made by immigrants for Sam, and imaginary for Major Preston.
jokes.setup: Jenny distributing customized cookies
jokes.punchline: Escalating from dietary to racist to completely delusional needs
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from normal (dietary) to problematic (racist) to absurd (imaginary). Shows different types of elderly issues with efficient comedy.

jokes.text: With this cookie, I can buy back Private O'Malley from the Zulus.
jokes.setup: Major Preston receiving his imaginary cookie
jokes.punchline: His specific delusion about military transactions
jokes.explanation: Specific military delusion feels authentic to the character. The Zulu detail adds historical absurdity.

jokes.text: You put ex-lax in the cookies, then just sit back and watch the fireworks? I would never do that! Oh, right. I was thinking of other things that are hilarious. Like things that you think are funny.
jokes.setup: Bart suspicious of Jenny's kindness
jokes.punchline: His awkward backpedal when she's genuinely nice
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's cynicism clashing with genuine goodness. The backpedal is awkward but runs too long and gets confusing.

jokes.text: She had more curves than a Hot Wheels track. Well, she didn't yet, but she would after puberty.
jokes.setup: Bart's film noir narration about attraction
jokes.punchline: Realistic correction about age-appropriate development
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of film noir clichés with age-appropriate reality. The Hot Wheels metaphor is perfect for a kid, and the correction is both realistic and funny.

jokes.text: Math, done. Pinecone, collected. Permission slip, signed. Now time for a break, a little social studies.
jokes.setup: Lisa efficiently completing tasks
jokes.punchline: Her idea of a break is more academic work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa characterization - her break is more studying. Predictable but true to character.

jokes.text: Whoa! She gets to use the real scissors. Nice.
jokes.setup: Nelson commenting on Bart's crush
jokes.punchline: Fifth grade scissors as impressive status symbol
jokes.explanation: Captures elementary school social hierarchy perfectly. Real scissors are genuinely impressive to younger kids.

jokes.text: Punch her in her arm. Or if you're really serious, stick gum in her hair. Here, I always come prepared. Take two. You might get lucky.
jokes.setup: Nelson giving romantic advice
jokes.punchline: His increasingly destructive courtship suggestions with prepared gum
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson - aggressive, misguided, but oddly prepared. The escalation and 'come prepared' detail makes it work.

jokes.text: Boy, you make it look effortless. And that kid's giving you zero pump.
jokes.setup: Nelson watching Bart talk to Jenny
jokes.punchline: His sports commentary on social interaction
jokes.explanation: Nelson treating conversation like sports coaching. 'Zero pump' is oddly specific sports terminology applied to romance.

jokes.text: You'd love my PB & J. The secret is no PB, double J.
jokes.setup: Bart asking Jenny on a picnic
jokes.punchline: His sandwich is just jelly
jokes.explanation: Economical joke about poor/lazy food prep presented as culinary innovation. The abbreviation format makes it snappy.

jokes.text: Willie, I've got a girlfriend! Really? Well, I do, too. She's a bikini model from Sweden.
jokes.setup: Bart excitedly sharing his news
jokes.punchline: Willie's obvious lie in response
jokes.explanation: Classic lonely guy fantasy response. Predictable but fits Willie's character of trying to maintain dignity.

jokes.text: Willie, darling, come back to bed. Don't you love me? Don't compete with my job, Inga. You'll never win.
jokes.setup: Willie's imaginary girlfriend calling to him
jokes.punchline: His commitment to work over his fake relationship
jokes.explanation: Commits to the bit by having Willie argue with his imaginary girlfriend. His work dedication even extends to fake relationships.

jokes.text: In 50 years, the vacuum cleaner will be quiet and not scary.
jokes.setup: Ralph giving his future prediction
jokes.punchline: His innocent, personal hope about vacuum cleaners
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph - innocent, personal, and completely missing the assignment's scope. His simple fear is endearingly childlike.

jokes.text: Oh, Ralph, how I envy your optimism. There is no Springfield 50 years in the future!
jokes.setup: Lisa responding to Ralph's innocent prediction
jokes.punchline: Her apocalyptic environmental despair
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Ralph's innocence and Lisa's catastrophic thinking. Shows her character growth into environmental activism.

jokes.text: With global warming trapping the CO2 inside our poisonous atmosphere, our super-heated oceans will rise, drowning our lowlands, leaving what's left of humanity baking in deserts that once fed the world!
jokes.setup: Lisa presenting after Ralph's innocent essay
jokes.punchline: Her apocalyptic environmental rant completely opposite in tone
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition with Ralph. Classic Lisa environmental passion taken to extreme.

jokes.text: Lisa, your outburst was either a sign of deep emotional imbalance or a passionate response to a sobering truth. Luckily, the treatment for both is intensive therapy.
jokes.setup: Teacher responding to Lisa's environmental breakdown
jokes.punchline: Her casual diagnosis and treatment recommendation
jokes.explanation: Great structure - acknowledges both possibilities but offers the same solution. The casual 'luckily' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't know. But when you go, could you return this Sports Illustrated for me? Someone must've put it in my bag. They do that, you know? They're always putting things in my bag.
jokes.setup: Teacher responding to Lisa's therapy question
jokes.punchline: Her obvious lie about the magazine ownership
jokes.explanation: Teacher's embarrassing denial about owning Sports Illustrated. The escalating explanation makes it more obvious. Runs a bit long.

jokes.text: I spend a lot of time working with this mentally challenged guy. Why, here he is now. Looking good, Homer! Just wait.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to impress Jenny with his charity work
jokes.punchline: Describing his own father as mentally challenged
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of Bart exploiting his father's stupidity for social credit. The casual cruelty is both shocking and funny.

jokes.text: Don't shake your fist at me! Look, man. I don't want any trouble. Come on. Here. Just take my wallet. Leave me alone, okay?
jokes.setup: Homer encountering what appears to be a threatening situation
jokes.punchline: His immediate cowardly surrender to unknown threat
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer cowardice - immediate surrender without assessing the situation. Predictable but true to character.

jokes.text: Do you know who else was really into rowboats? Jesus. And he could've turned his rowboat into a jet ski, but he didn't. Good guy.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to make conversation while rowing
jokes.punchline: His theological take on Jesus's transportation choices
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd theological observation. The idea that Jesus chose humility over jet skis is both ridiculous and oddly respectful. Great economy.

jokes.text: Oh. You want your duckling back? Here it is. Psych! That'll teach you to match wits with us, you stupid mother duck!
jokes.setup: Bart with a duckling and mother duck
jokes.punchline: His cruel taunting of a duck with 'psych'
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's casual cruelty and childish taunting. 'Match wits' with a duck is absurd but the cruelty undermines the humor slightly.

jokes.text: Either the duck suffers or the kid does or none of you's getting nothing from Shauna. I'm Shauna.
jokes.setup: Bullies explaining their motivation
jokes.punchline: Girl's casual introduction after describing violence terms
jokes.explanation: The casual self-introduction after describing violence terms is well-timed. Shows the twisted social dynamics of bullying.

jokes.text: Oh, Bart, if those ducklings understood how magnificent you were, they'd stop biting your leg.
jokes.setup: Jenny watching Bart's heroic rescue attempt
jokes.punchline: Her romantic interpretation of him being attacked by ducks
jokes.explanation: Perfect romantic delusion - she sees his failure as noble. The ducks biting him while she swoons is great visual irony.

jokes.text: Let me show you how Ignorital works. It turns this to this! Huh?
jokes.setup: Doctor demonstrating antidepressant effects
jokes.punchline: Visual transformation (presumably sad to happy imagery)
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about medication marketing. Without seeing the actual transformation, it's hard to assess fully. Likely a simple before/after visual.

jokes.text: Lisa's a little young to be taking happy pills. Marge, we came to an appointment in the middle of the day. That's the most a parent can do.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing concern about medicating Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer's low bar for parental involvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - showing up is maximum parental effort. Dark commentary on modern parenting and medical solutions.

jokes.text: What's your angle, mister? No angle. If this girl I'm seeing comes here, and she might, I wouldn't mind if you told her I was the kind of boy that does the dishes.
jokes.setup: Marge suspicious of Bart's helpfulness
jokes.punchline: His calculated attempt to manufacture a good reputation
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's calculating nature and Marge's justified suspicion. The careful phrasing 'and she might' shows his uncertainty.

jokes.text: Yeah. Busboys get all the chicks.
jokes.setup: Bart's domestic strategy
jokes.punchline: Marge's dry observation about dish-washing appeal
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Marge's practical wisdom delivered with sarcasm. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Well, off to school where they'll prepare our little minds for a future we'll never see. Sounds like someone's got a case of the Mondays.
jokes.setup: Lisa's apocalyptic outlook on education
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual corporate cliché response
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Lisa's existential dread and Homer's casual dismissal. The 'case of the Mondays' reference shows his obliviousness.

jokes.text: So this is setting the table. If I'd known how easy this was, I'd have just done it instead of throwing all those tantrums.
jokes.setup: Bart doing household chores
jokes.punchline: His realization that compliance is easier than rebellion
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Bart discovering that cooperation is actually easier. Relatable parenting moment.

jokes.text: Oh, Bart, I don't care that this is just an act. You've finally become the boy every mother dreams of, a girl!
jokes.setup: Marge appreciating Bart's helpful behavior
jokes.punchline: Her ultimate compliment is that he's become feminine
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline structure with great misdirection. Marge's honest assessment of gender differences in household help is both funny and pointed.

jokes.text: Hello, smiley face. Hello, smiley face!
jokes.setup: Bart asking about his family being okay
jokes.punchline: Lisa on medication greeting everything with artificial happiness
jokes.explanation: Standard medication side effects humor. Shows the drug's effect but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Jenny, it's amazing what you've done with my son. Well, I think Bart is an incredible combination of Habitat for Humanity and the Jonas Brothers, and nothing can ever change my opinion about him.
jokes.setup: Marge praising Jenny's influence
jokes.punchline: Jenny's oddly specific celebrity comparison and absolute certainty
jokes.explanation: The Jonas Brothers comparison is oddly specific and the absolute certainty is ominous foreshadowing. Decent setup for the inevitable revelation.

jokes.text: We don't have any money! Go away! May I de-corn your cob, my darling?
jokes.setup: Homer answering the door defensively
jokes.punchline: Immediate switch to romantic corn preparation offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - from aggressive to romantic in one beat. 'De-corn your cob' is both sweet and slightly ridiculous phrasing.

jokes.text: Bart, I learned some great new swear words from my uncle. He has got a broken back, so he's in constant pain. Bart, would you be interested in swears?
jokes.setup: Milhouse excited to share something with Bart
jokes.punchline: His uncle's medical condition as source of vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny connection between chronic pain and creative profanity. Milhouse's innocent enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yes, because only by knowing them can you prevent bad little kids from saying them.
jokes.setup: Milhouse offering to teach swear words
jokes.punchline: Bart's fake noble justification for learning profanity
jokes.explanation: Classic kid logic for justifying bad behavior. Bart's fake righteousness while maintaining his good boy act.

jokes.text: Milhouse, real best friends often don't speak for years at a time, like John Adams and Thomas Jefferson. Just because you saw one episode of one miniseries doesn't mean you get to use it as a whip!
jokes.setup: Bart defending his absence with historical parallel
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's specific TV knowledge accusation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from historical reference to media criticism. 'Use it as a whip' is perfect phrasing. Shows both characters' personalities perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, I get it. It's love. I guess you've found your Abigail Adams. My who? So I'll be on my way. I can see I'm the fifth wheel on this bicycle with training wheels.
jokes.setup: Milhouse understanding the romantic situation
jokes.punchline: His mixed metaphor about being unwanted
jokes.explanation: Great mixed metaphor - fifth wheel on a training wheel bicycle is both mathematically wrong and emotionally right. Perfect Milhouse awkwardness.

jokes.text: Reverend, care for some of my devil's food cake? Is that really devil's food? No. It's angel food with chocolate on top. Mmm-hmm. I knew it.
jokes.setup: Ned offering cake to the reverend
jokes.punchline: Religious concern about cake naming and Ned's workaround
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders - finding religious loopholes for dessert naming. Reverend's knowing response shows he's dealt with this before.

jokes.text: We should be safe from Milhouse here. Being at sea level gives him nosebleeds. Milhouse is funny. The way he's always shaking me by the shoulders and saying I don't know the real you. Funny.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why they're safe from Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Jenny's innocent observation about Milhouse's dramatic behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Milhouse's physical weakness. Jenny's innocent misreading of his desperation as humor shows her naivety.

jokes.text: Well, you can't bleed out your nose when your heart's broken.
jokes.setup: Milhouse appearing despite the sea level issue
jokes.punchline: His poetic explanation for overcoming physical limitations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line - poetic, heartbreaking, and perfectly Milhouse. The medical/emotional logic is both absurd and touching. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I'm only pretending to be good. Before I met you, I was El Barto, Skinner's Bane, the Sultan of Spit Wads, the Dean of D-Minus, the Ay Caramba Kid!
jokes.setup: Bart finally confessing to Jenny
jokes.punchline: His elaborate list of villainous titles
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of increasingly ridiculous titles. 'Dean of D-Minus' and 'Ay Caramba Kid' are particularly inspired. Shows Bart's theatrical nature.

jokes.text: Are you saying our entire relationship is based on lies? Not our entire relationship. Just the stuff I said.
jokes.setup: Jenny's accusation about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Bart's literal and unhelpful clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline structure. Bart's distinction between relationship and words is both logically absurd and emotionally devastating. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Whatever you are looking for, it is not at the bottom of a 512-ounce cup of Shrek Berry Blast.
jokes.setup: Apu counseling depressed Bart
jokes.punchline: His wisdom about excessive drink consumption
jokes.explanation: Good Apu wisdom with specific product details. The 512-ounce size is absurd but believable for convenience store culture.

jokes.text: What is at the bottom is this dumb Shrek trivia quiz, which if answered correctly, entitles you to 10% off any Shrek DVD, except the first two.
jokes.setup: Apu continuing his drink wisdom
jokes.punchline: The disappointing reality of promotional contests
jokes.explanation: Good observation about disappointing promotional offers. The 'except the first two' detail is realistic and frustrating.

jokes.text: Okay, I... What happened to my roses, Apu? You said you'd watch them while I shopped. No such conversation took place.
jokes.setup: Bart discovering his roses are gone
jokes.punchline: Apu's complete denial of responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu - technically honest but unhelpful. His formal denial contrasts with Bart's casual assumption.

jokes.text: Grandma, a lot has happened. I went to a condo presentation. I put down my deposit. But then they lost funding mid-project. That crane just sits there every day, rusting.
jokes.setup: Customer talking to grandmother's grave
jokes.punchline: His mundane financial disappointment as major life event
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - treating financial disappointment as major life update for deceased grandmother. Feels authentic.

jokes.text: Smooth and slippery. Thank you, Zamboni King.
jokes.setup: Setting up ice rink prank
jokes.punchline: Willie's proud professional title for ice maintenance
jokes.explanation: Willie's pride in his custodial work elevated to royal status. 'Zamboni King' is a great humble-brag title.
